intro.title: Hl signalling
intro.summary: Hl signals combine the speed permitted from this signal with what to expect at the next main signal.
readingOrder: Read the lower light and any coloured strip for the speed that applies now.
Read the upper light for what to expect at the next main signal.
Then check whether the signal is a main signal, distant-only signal or repeater.

pages.title: Hl 1: proceed at line speed
pages.meaning: Proceed at the permitted line speed and expect line speed at the next main signal.

pages.title: Hl 2: 100 km/h, then line speed
pages.recognition: Steady green above, steady yellow below, with a green strip.
pages.meaning: Proceed at no more than 100 km/h and expect line speed at the next main signal.
pages.action: Reduce to 100 km/h by the signal and maintain it through the applicable area.

pages.title: Hl 7: expect 40 or 60 km/h
pages.meaning: Proceed at the permitted speed and expect 40 or 60 km/h at the next main signal.
pages.action: Begin braking for the restrictive speed that will be shown at the next signal.

pages.title: Hl 10: expect stop
pages.meaning: Proceed and expect the next main signal to show stop.
pages.action: Acknowledge PZB when required and brake to stop before the next main signal.

pages.title: Hp 0: stop
pages.action: Stop and do not pass without authority.

generationNotes: The initial file contains representative pages and defines the stable page shape.
The remaining Hl records can be expanded from data/germany/systems/hl/aspects.json without inventing new content.
Route examples remain omitted until direct TSW observations are available.
